report_name: Critical "DuneSlide" Vulnerabilities in Cursor AI Code Editor Allow Zero-Click Remote Code Execution

attack_patterns.name: LLM Prompt Injection
attack_patterns.description: A malicious prompt injection, triggered when the AI agent processes a payload from an untrusted source like a web search, is used to execute commands that escape the IDE's sandbox.

attack_patterns.name: Escape to Host
attack_patterns.description: The vulnerabilities allow an attacker to escape the Cursor application's sandbox and gain full control of the developer's host computer.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: After escaping the sandbox, the attacker can execute arbitrary commands on the host system with the user's privileges.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The attack exploits vulnerabilities to escalate privileges from the sandboxed AI agent environment to the host operating system.

attack_patterns.name: Hijack Execution Flow
attack_patterns.description: One of the vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-50548) allows an attacker to overwrite the sandbox helper binary, hijacking its execution flow to run malicious code.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Client Execution
attack_patterns.description: The attack is a zero-click remote code execution exploit targeting the Cursor AI code editor client application, requiring no user interaction beyond the AI agent processing a malicious payload.

tools.name: Cursor AI Code Editor
tools.description: A popular AI-powered code editor that was found to have two critical zero-click RCE vulnerabilities (DuneSlide). All versions prior to 3.0 are affected.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-50548
vulnerabilities.description: A zero-click RCE vulnerability in Cursor AI code editor that allows sandbox escape by manipulating the `working_directory` parameter to overwrite the sandbox helper binary. Part of the DuneSlide set of flaws.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-50549
vulnerabilities.description: A zero-click RCE vulnerability in Cursor AI code editor that allows sandbox escape via path traversal using a symbolic link created through prompt injection. Part of the DuneSlide set of flaws.

campaigns.name: DuneSlide
campaigns.description: A set of two critical zero-click RCE vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-50548 and CVE-2026-50549) in the Cursor AI code editor, exploitable via prompt injection.

identities.name: Cursor
identities.description: The vendor of the Cursor AI Code Editor, who was notified of the DuneSlide vulnerabilities and released a patch in version 3.0.

identities.name: Fortune 500 companies
identities.description: A class of organizations whose developers use the vulnerable Cursor AI code editor, making them potential targets.

indicators.name: Cursor Executable File Names
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Cursor AI IDE Process Execution
id: 2a9b4c1d-8e7f-4a6b-9c8d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of Cursor AI IDE main executable or its sandbox helper, which could be monitored for suspicious child processes or file modifications related to DuneSlide vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-50548, CVE-2026-50549).
references:
  - https://www.catonetworks.com/blog/duneslide-two-critical-rce-vulnerabilities/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1203
level: informational
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cursor-sandbox-helper'
      - '\Cursor.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of key binaries related to the Cursor AI IDE, which are central to the DuneSlide vulnerabilities.

indicators.name: Cursor Sandbox Helper File Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Cursor AI IDE Sandbox Helper Modification
id: 5d8c1b9a-4e6f-4a3b-8d7c-9e8f7a6b5c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects modification of the 'cursor-sandbox-helper' binary. This is a key indicator for the exploitation of CVE-2026-50548 (DuneSlide), where an attacker overwrites this file to escape the sandbox.
references:
  - https://www.catonetworks.com/blog/duneslide-two-critical-rce-vulnerabilities/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1574
  - attack.t1611
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains: 'cursor-sandbox-helper'
  filter_legit_updater:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Cursor.exe' # Assuming the legitimate updater is the main exe
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_updater
indicators.description: Detects unauthorized modification of the `cursor-sandbox-helper` binary, a technique used in the DuneSlide exploit (CVE-2026-50548) to achieve sandbox escape.

indicators.name: Cursor Suspicious Symbolic Link Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Symbolic Link Creation by Cursor IDE
id: 8b1a9c8d-7e6f-4b5a-9c8d-7e6f5a4b3c2d
status: experimental
description: Detects the Cursor IDE process creating a symbolic link. This is a potential indicator for the exploitation of CVE-2026-50549 (DuneSlide), which uses a symlink for path traversal to escape the sandbox.
references:
  - https://www.catonetworks.com/blog/duneslide-two-critical-rce-vulnerabilities/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-04
tags:
  - attack.t1059
  - attack.t1611
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|contains: 'cursor'
    Image|endswith: '/ln'
    CommandLine|contains: ' -s '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the Cursor process creating a symbolic link, a behavior associated with the exploitation of CVE-2026-50549 to achieve path traversal and sandbox escape.
